subject: M Refinement doesn't improve membrane protein resolution
body:
Hi all,  

I’ve been processing a dataset using warp-relion: a 180Å 
diameter membrane protein with high symmetry. Subtomos are at a pixel size 
of 3.6 Å/pix. collected on a Talos with a K2, dose-symmetric tilt scheme, 
125 total dose e¯/Å², 3° tilt steps, 8 frames/movie (0.38 e¯/Å²/frame). The 
protocol I’ve used so far is as follows: Tomo reconstruction in AreTomo, 
Manual particle picking in imod, CTF correction and particle extraction in 
warp. I first extracted at a pixel size of 10Å, classified and refined till 
I reached the Nyquist of that pixel size, then put it through M and 
extracted at a 7.5Å. I repeated this till I was at the smallest pixel size 
of the data. 

Using this I’ve gotten both this dataset to about 11A, but I am struggling 
to get it any further. One of the major concerns I’ve had is that 
refinement in M has never significantly improved my resolution. My 
resolution always only improves marginally (e.g. 13.2 to 13), stays the 
same or drops significantly. 

I’ve also tried another dataset to check of the problem is down to the 
dataset or the collection settings (Protein lattice on a membrane, 2.2 
Å/pix, Krios with a K3, total dose 60 e¯/Å², 3° tilt steps, 10 frames/movie 
(0.146 e¯/Å²/frame). However, the results have been very similar and 
despite being able to increase resolution in relion, the M refinement 
doesn’t help.  

I was wondering what the possible reasons are for this and what 
troubleshooting steps I can take to improve this. Could this be due to the 
fact they’re both membrane proteins? I’ve tried masking the membrane out as 
much as I can, but do you have any other tips for membrane proteins. I’ve 
tried the troubleshooting suggestions on previous posts (reverting metadata 
to pre-M, reconstructing subtomos from warp, refining a simplified model) 
but without any luck so far. 

Many thanks in advance! 

Kain
